title: Germany Faces Political Struggles and Industrial Changes
subtitle: Chancellor Merz deals with coalition tensions while BioNTech reshapes its future amid global climate efforts.

summary.en: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz marks one year in office amid political friction within his coalition government. His administration struggles to pass major reforms as internal disagreements over economic policy grow. Meanwhile, the pharmaceutical company BioNTech is closing its COVID vaccine production in Germany, risking thousands of jobs. The company is shifting its focus to cancer research despite rising financial losses. In a broader European context, cities like Amsterdam are banning ads for fossil fuels and meat to fight climate change.
summary.zh: 德國總理弗里德里希·梅爾茨（Friedrich Merz）在聯合政府內部的政治摩擦中迎來上任一週年。隨著對經濟政策的內部分歧加劇，他的政府難以通過重大改革。與此同時，藥廠 BioNTech 正在關閉其在德國的新冠疫苗生產線，數千個工作崗位面臨風險。儘管財務虧損增加，該公司正將重點轉向癌症研究。在更廣泛的歐洲背景下，阿姆斯特丹等城市正禁止化石燃料和肉類廣告以對抗氣候變遷。

paragraphs.en: German Chancellor Friedrich Merz is facing a difficult first year in office. His coalition government, which includes the conservative Christian Democrats (CDU), the Christian Social Union (CSU), and the center-left Social Democrats (SPD), is experiencing significant tension. Merz recently admitted that there is growing resentment within his own party about the compromises they must make to stay in power. His personal approval ratings are also very low, reflecting the public's frustration with the political instability.
paragraphs.zh: 德國總理弗里德里希·梅爾茨（Friedrich Merz）面臨著上任後艱難的第一年。他的聯合政府包含保守派的基督教民主聯盟（CDU）、基督教社會聯盟（CSU）以及中間偏左的社會民主黨（SPD），目前正經歷嚴重的內部緊張。梅爾茨最近承認，為了維持執政地位而必須做出的妥協，讓他自己黨內的不滿情緒日益升高。他的個人支持率也非常低，反映出公眾對政治不穩定的不滿。

paragraphs.en: The challenges began even before Merz officially took office. When he was elected as the 10th German chancellor in May 2025, he did not receive the necessary majority in the first round of voting. This was a historic moment, showing that his coalition holds only a thin majority in parliament. To become chancellor, he had to win a second round of voting. This narrow victory highlights the fragile nature of the current political landscape in Germany.
paragraphs.zh: 挑戰甚至在梅爾茨正式上任前就已開始。當他在 2025 年 5 月當選為第 10 任德國總理時，他在第一輪投票中並未獲得所需的過半數支持。這是一個歷史性時刻，顯示他的聯盟在議會中僅佔微弱多數。為了成為總理，他必須贏得第二輪投票。這次險勝凸顯了德國當前政治版圖的脆弱性。

paragraphs.en: Merz had promised an "autumn of reforms" in late 2025. He planned to change fundamental parts of the German welfare state, including pensions, healthcare, and nursing care. He also wanted to reduce bureaucracy, which is the complex system of rules and paperwork that businesses and citizens must follow. However, none of these major changes were confirmed in the following months. Instead, the government focused on a €500 billion special fund for infrastructure and climate neutrality by 2045. This money is intended for projects like upgrading the rail network and building new bridges, as Germany struggles with old and crumbling infrastructure.
paragraphs.zh: 梅爾茨曾承諾在 2025 年底推動「改革之秋」。他計劃改變德國福利國家的基礎結構，包括退休金、醫療保健和護理服務。他也希望減少官僚主義，也就是企業和公民必須遵循的複雜規則與文件程序。然而，這些重大變革在隨後的幾個月中均未獲確認。相反地，政府將重點放在一項 5,000 億歐元的特別基金上，用於基礎建設和到 2045 年實現氣候中立。这笔資金旨在用於升級鐵路網絡和建造新橋樑等項目，因為德國正苦於老舊且破敗的基礎設施。

paragraphs.en: A major source of conflict is the relationship between the finance minister, Lars Klingbeil, and the minister for economic affairs, Katherina Reiche. Reiche wants to reverse many policies of the previous government, which combined economic growth with climate protection. She is also pushing for the return of civilian nuclear power, which was phased out in 2011. Merz has suggested that his party is bearing the brunt of the compromises, describing the coalition as having a "larger partner and a smaller partner." This comment has been seen as a way to warn his own party that they need to be more flexible.
paragraphs.zh: 衝突的主要來源之一是財政部長拉爾斯·克林拜爾（Lars Klingbeil）與經濟事務部長卡塔里娜·賴歇（Katherina Reiche）之間的關係。賴歇希望推翻前任政府的許多政策，該政策將經濟成長與氣候保護相結合。她也在推動恢復民用核能，該能源在 2011 年已被逐步淘汰。梅爾茨暗示他的黨派承擔了妥協的主要負擔，將聯盟描述為「一個較大的夥伴和一個較小的夥伴」。這項評論被視為他在警告自己的黨派需要更加靈活。

paragraphs.en: In the healthcare sector, the coalition has presented plans to reform the health system, but nothing has been decided yet. There will be several months of discussion in the Bundestag, during which changes are expected. DW Chief Political Editor Michaela Küfner notes that while Merz’s start has been halting, it is not entirely negative. He has managed to keep the coalition together despite the difficulties.
paragraphs.zh: 在醫療保健領域，聯盟已提出改革醫療體系的計劃，但尚未做出任何決定。聯邦議會（Bundestag）將進行數個月的討論，期間預計將會有變動。德國之聲（DW）首席政治編輯米歇拉·庫夫納（Michaela Küfner）指出，雖然梅爾茨的起步有些遲緩，但並非完全負面。他成功地在困難中維持了聯盟的團結。

paragraphs.en: In the industrial sector, BioNTech, the Mainz-based company famous for its COVID-19 vaccine, is making drastic changes. The company announced it is shutting down its COVID vaccine production entirely in Germany. This decision leaves the production to its US partner, Pfizer. BioNTech is closing production centers in Idar-Oberstein, Marburg, Tübingen, and Singapore. The closures are expected to be completed by the end of 2027.
paragraphs.zh: 在工業領域，位於美因茨（Mainz）的知名新冠疫苗公司 BioNTech 正在進行劇變。該公司宣布將完全停止在德國生產新冠疫苗。這項決定將生產工作留給其美國夥伴輝瑞（Pfizer）。BioNTech 正在關閉位於伊達爾-奧伯施泰因（Idar-Oberstein）、馬爾堡（Marburg）、蒂賓根（Tübingen）和新加坡的生產中心。這些關閉作業預計將在 2027 年底完成。

paragraphs.en: This restructuring puts up to 1,860 jobs at risk. BioNTech had previously planned to cut between 950 and 1,350 jobs. The company is moving away from COVID treatments because demand has dropped significantly. Now, vaccines are mainly given to the elderly and high-risk people. BioNTech is redirecting its focus to cancer research and other illnesses. However, this shift is costly. The company reported falling revenues and rising net losses in the first quarter of 2026. These losses are due to investments in new research, including an antibody called Pumitamig, which is currently in global phase 2 trials. The company’s founders, Ugur Sahin and Özlem Türeci, also announced they are stepping down to start a new venture.
paragraphs.zh: 這項重組使高達 1,860 個工作崗位面臨風險。BioNTech 此前曾計劃裁員 950 至 1,350 人。由於需求大幅下降，該公司正撤離新冠治療領域。目前，疫苗主要接種給老年人和高風險人群。BioNTech 正將其重點轉向癌症研究和其他疾病。然而，這種轉變代價高昂。該公司報告稱，2026 年第一季度營收下滑且淨虧損增加。這些虧損源於對新研究的投資，包括一種名為 Pumitamig 的抗體，目前該抗體正處於全球二期試驗階段。該公司的創始人烏古爾·沙欣（Ugur Sahin）和奧茲勒姆·圖雷奇（Özlem Türeci）也宣布將辭職，以創辦新事業。

paragraphs.en: While Germany deals with internal political and industrial shifts, other European cities are taking bold steps against climate change. Amsterdam has become the first capital city in the world to ban advertisements for meat and fossil fuel products. This includes ads for cars, flights, cruises, and home gas heating. The ban aims to reduce the normalization of high-carbon lifestyles. Reint Jan Renes, a behavioral psychologist, supports this move. He argues that it is inconsistent for a city to have ambitious climate goals while allowing ads that promote pollution.
paragraphs.zh: 當德國處理內部政治和產業變遷時，其他歐洲城市正採取大膽步驟對抗氣候變遷。阿姆斯特丹成為全球第一個禁止肉類和化石燃料產品廣告的首都。這包括汽車、航班、郵輪和家庭燃氣供暖的廣告。這項禁令旨在減少高碳生活方式的正常化。行為心理學家雷因特·揚·雷內斯（Reint Jan Renes）支持這項舉措。他認為，一個城市在擁有雄心勃勃的氣候目標的同時卻允許推廣污染的廣告，這是不一致的。

paragraphs.en: Researchers estimate that car and airline ads in the EU in 2019 alone could be responsible for up to 122 million tons of greenhouse gas emissions. By banning these ads, cities hope to change public attitudes toward fossil fuel consumption. This approach is similar to the bans on tobacco advertising in the 20th century, which successfully reduced smoking rates. Stockholm will follow Amsterdam’s lead this summer, and more than 50 other cities worldwide have similar bans. This trend suggests a growing global effort to address climate change through public policy and advertising restrictions.
paragraphs.zh: 研究人員估計，僅 2019 年歐盟境內的汽車和航空公司廣告就可能導致高達 1.22 億噸的溫室氣體排放。透過禁止這些廣告，城市希望改變公眾對化石燃料消費的態度。這種做法類似於 20 世紀對菸草廣告的禁令，成功降低了吸菸率。斯德哥爾摩將在今年夏天跟隨阿姆斯特丹的步伐，全球還有超過 50 個城市實施類似的禁令。這種趨勢顯示出透過公共政策和廣告限制來應對氣候變遷的全球努力正在增長。

keyTerms.definitionEn: To suffer the worst part of something unpleasant, such as criticism, punishment, or negative effects.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 承受最嚴重的後果或衝擊。
keyTerms.noteZh: brunt 原意為「衝擊」，bear the brunt 指承受主要壓力。文中：describing the coalition as having a "larger partner and a smaller partner." This comment has been seen as a way to warn his own party that they need to be more flexible. Merz has suggested that his party is bearing the brunt of the compromises

keyTerms.definitionEn: Not smooth or steady; hesitant or irregular in progress.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 斷斷續續的；不穩定的；猶豫不決的。
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見作形容詞表示「停頓的」或「結巴的」，這裡形容進展不順利、充滿停頓。文中：DW Chief Political Editor Michaela Küfner notes that while Merz’s start has been halting, it is not entirely negative.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To resign from a job or position of authority.
keyTerms.noteZh: step down 是固定片語，指主動離開職位，並非字面上的「走下」。文中：The company’s founders, Ugur Sahin and Özlem Türeci, also announced they are stepping down to start a new venture.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To gradually stop using or producing something over a period of time.
keyTerms.definitionZh: 逐步淘汰；分階段停止使用。
keyTerms.noteZh: phase out 指有計劃地、逐步地移除某事物，而非突然禁止。文中：She is also pushing for the return of civilian nuclear power, which was phased out in 2011.

sources.title: Here's why Amsterdam banned fossil fuel and meat advertising

quiz.question: Why did Friedrich Merz require a second round of voting to become Chancellor?

options.A: Because the coalition government had not yet been officially formed.
options.B: Because he failed to secure a majority in the first round of voting.
options.C: Because the parliament rejected his initial proposal for infrastructure funding.
options.D: Because he needed time to negotiate with the Christian Social Union (CSU).

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案 B，因為文章第二段明確指出，Merz 在 2025 年 5 月當選時，並未在第一輪投票中獲得所需的過半數支持（did not receive the necessary majority in the first round of voting），因此必須進行第二輪投票才能正式成為總理。選項 A 錯誤，因為聯盟政府在他當選前已存在；選項 C 錯誤，因為基礎設施資金是後續的議題，並非當選條件；選項 D 錯誤，文章未提及他需要時間與 CSU 談判才能當選，而是因為票數不足。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the stability of Merz’s coalition government based on his comment about a 'larger partner and a smaller partner'?

options.A: The government is unified and strongly supports the finance minister's policies.
options.B: The coalition is fragile, and Merz's party is feeling the pressure of necessary compromises.
options.C: The smaller partner holds more power than the larger partner in economic decisions.
options.D: Merz intends to dissolve the coalition and form a new government with different parties.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案 B，根據文章第四段，Merz 提到聯盟有「較大夥伴和較小夥伴」，並被視為警告其政黨需要更靈活（flexible）。結合第一段提到的內部不滿（resentment）和第二段提到的微弱多數（thin majority），可推論出聯盟並不穩固，且 Merz 所屬政黨正承受妥協的壓力。選項 A 錯誤，因為文章強調的是緊張關係而非統一；選項 C 錯誤，文章未提及較小夥伴擁有更多權力；選項 D 錯誤，文章第五段指出 Merz 成功維持了聯盟的完整，並無解散之意。

quiz.question: In the context of the article, what does the word 'halting' most likely mean when describing Merz’s start as Chancellor?
quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案 B，在第五段中，DW 政治編輯 Michaela Küfner 指出 Merz 的起步是「halting」，但並非完全負面。結合前文提到的政治不穩定、妥協困難以及改革延宕，「halting」在此處意指「斷斷續續的」或「猶豫不穩的」，形容進展不順暢。選項 A 錯誤，文章並未強調其突然性；選項 C 錯誤，因為文章描述的是挑戰和延誤，而非高效；選項 D 錯誤，雖然結果是維持了聯盟，但過程被描述為充滿困難，並非完全成功。

quiz.question: Which of the following best summarizes the main idea of the article?

options.A: BioNTech's strategic shift from COVID vaccines to cancer research is the most significant event in Germany.
options.B: European cities are successfully eliminating all forms of fossil fuel advertising to combat climate change.
options.C: Germany faces political instability and industrial restructuring, while Europe adopts new measures against climate change.
options.D: Friedrich Merz has successfully implemented all his promised reforms within his first year in office.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案 C，文章前半部分詳細描述了德國總理 Merz 面臨的政治挑戰、聯盟的緊張關係以及 BioNTech 的產業重組；後半部分則轉向歐洲其他城市（如阿姆斯特丹和斯德哥爾摩）採取的禁絕化石燃料廣告的氣候變遷對策。選項 A 僅涵蓋了產業部分，忽略了政治和氣候議題；選項 B 僅涵蓋了氣候議題，且「eliminating all forms」過於絕對；選項 D 錯誤，因為文章明確指出 Merz 承諾的改革並未在首月確認，且政治局勢依然緊張。
